value.found_quote: Containers (Docker) | Setup dependent | Low | Medium ... Containers provide isolation through Linux namespaces. Each container has its own view of the filesystem, process tree, and network stack, while sharing the host kernel. A security-hardened container configuration might look like this: docker run --cap-drop ALL --security-opt no-new-privileges --security-opt seccomp=/path/to/seccomp-profile.json --read-only ... --network none ... -v /path/to/code:/workspace:ro
value.rationale: The page lists "Containers (Docker)" as an isolation technology for running Claude Code or the Agent SDK, describing isolation through Linux namespaces, a security-hardened configuration with seccomp profiles, dropped capabilities, network policies (--network none, proxy), and mounted volumes — matching all elements of the claim.
